title: Decoder-Only: Autoregressive Generation
concept: Decoder-only models use causal masking to generate text step-by-step, becoming the standard for modern LLMs.

exercises.prompt: How do decoder-only language models generate a.decoder-only models generate a response to your favorite AI models with a decoder-only models generate sentences generate text?

options.text: Backwards, starting from the last word

exercises.explain: A decoder-only model generates text one word (or token) at a time, predicting the next word based on all the previous words.

cards.title: How AI Writes in Real-Time
cards.body: Ever wondered how ChatGPT writes its answers word by word right in front of your eyes? It is using a decoder-only architecture that guesses the very next word over and over again!

cards.title: Decoder-Only Model
cards.body: A decoder-only model is a type of neural network architecture that generates output text step-by-step (autoregressively). Unlike encoder-decoder models, it uses a single sequence and hides future words using a 'causal mask' so it only learns to predict what comes next.

cards.title: The Word-by-Word Writer
cards.body: Think of a decoder-only model like a writer trying to finish a sentence. They write one word, then read everything they have written so far, and use that context to write the very next word. They keep doing this, word by word, until the story is finished.

cards.title: Autoregressive Generation in Action
cards.body: When you ask an AI model to write a poem, it doesn't output the whole poem at once. It generates 'Once', then uses 'Once' to generate 'upon', then uses 'Once upon' to generate 'a', building the sentence token by token.

cards.title: Myth: AI Plans the Whole Sentence
cards.body: People often think that LLMs think of a whole paragraph first and then print it out. In reality, they have no idea what the end of the sentence will be; they only calculate the most likely next word based on what has already been written!

cards.title: Architectures Compared
cards.body: Let's compare how Decoder-Only architectures handle text generation compared to older Encoder-Decoder setups.

left.text: Uses two separate networks: one to analyze the input, and one to generate the output translation.

right.text: Uses one single network that processes the input and continues writing directly from it, step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: What does 'autoregressive' mean in the context of text generation?

options.text: It uses its own past outputs as inputs to generate the next word.
It generates all words in a sentence at the exact same millisecond.
It translates text into images before converting it back to words.
It only works when translating between two different languages.

exercises.explain: Autoregressive generation means the model uses its own previous outputs as the inputs for the next step, generating text one token at a time.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Most modern conversational AI assistants (like GPT-4) are built using decoder-only architectures.
exercises.explain: Modern LLMs like GPT-4 are decoder-only architectures because they are highly efficient at generating natural conversational text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to describe how decoder-only models hide future tokens during training.
exercises.sentence: Decoder-only models use a ___ mask to ensure they only look at past words when predicting the next word.
exercises.explain: Causal masking prevents the model from 'cheating' by looking ahead at future words during its training process.

exercises.prompt: Which of these processes describes an AI model using autoregressive generation?

options.text: A bot writing a story word by word, where each new word depends on the previous ones.
A database instantly retrieving a pre-written paragraph when a keyword is matched.
A search engine showing a list of links sorted by popularity.

exercises.explain: AI generation is done step-by-step, building on its own previous words. Copy-pasting or generating a full sentence instantly without step-by-step calculation is not how these models work.

exercises.prompt: Why have decoder-only models become the standard architecture for modern chat and text generation systems like ChatGPT?

options.text: They read the entire future sequence of words to plan out the perfect sentence.
They encode the text forwards and backwards at the same time to create summaries.
They mask out future words so they only learn to predict the next word using past words.
They use a separate encoder network to process the input before generating anything.

exercises.explain: Decoder-only models utilize causal masking during training to ensure they can only look at previous tokens when predicting the next one. This makes them highly specialized for autoregressive text generation, which is why they power the most famous modern LLMs like GPT.
